title: Chapter Ninety-Eight: The Thief Inside
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The idle wish was brief. He dismissed it.

This was a moment of glory as an individual warrior -- nearly impossible. The Divine Sword Group Leader had allowed access to his back. The vital point of one of the world's finest supreme masters. Would there be another chance to touch it?

*Swish.*

The sensation was rough where the Namgung Family Head's sword had torn through. Even as he placed his palm against the Group Leader's spine, the situation did not feel real. He felt detached.

*What am I doing here? Is this really happening?*

"From the vital point, go straight to the Body Pillar point," Yue Shou-lin whispered closely. "Standard Taoist small circulation will do. Our Group Leader has already opened all conception and governing vessels. Think of his whole-body meridians as blades. Think of it as oiling sword blades. That should be enough."

Her words carried insights about internal martial arts. Profound.

"They're coming! Coming this way! Really like vultures!"

"Blood demons first -- as expected, no respect. Next the Deep Martial Alliance, judging by their speed."

"Martial Dragon Association has changed formations. Battle formation."

He ignored the warnings. Buried his consciousness. Delved into the Divine Sword Group Leader's body along with his qi.

*Woong.*

He poured all his mental focus into a single stream of internal energy. Upper dantian ability activated -- the gift he had recently begun to consciously draw out. Even while running with lightness skills.

Yue Shou-lin's incredulous laugh brushed past his ears, then faded from consciousness.

And suddenly--

He felt what seemed like a universe.

The elasticity and breadth of the meridians were different from the start. The middle dantian closest to the vital point felt like a dragon's inner core.

*Is this... even human?*

Energy could flow in endlessly. He had not known a human body could be formed so profoundly. Deeply mysterious. But the blood flow was not normal -- the power that should have coursed like waves through twelve regular meridians, eight extraordinary vessels, and all the body's channels was faint. Rusted.

He infused the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique qi directly. Oiling the blade. He poured qi continuously from the vital point through to the Body Pillar point above. The internal power refined through the Jeong family method flowed as purely as the boy's childhood.

"What's this? It's as precise as if polished with a reverse muscle technique." The Group Leader's voice, bewildered. "Did the Shaolin Abbot secretly break his precepts? Are you perhaps his hidden son?"

Yeon-shin focused without minding it. The flow of energy, once momentum was gained, became rapid. A torrent.

The Group Leader's meridians were on a different level. Not just well-cultivated but smooth like North Sea ice walls. And there -- while observing the interior, Yeon-shin's upper dantian qi sense flashed. A heaven-given realm.

He discovered a very deep trace.

The pathway the Divine Sword Group Leader mainly used. Tremendously powerful energy had swept through like a typhoon. The qi marks were clear.

*This...*

He instinctively reverse-engineered the pathway's effectiveness. Then with a shudder, he realized.

This was energy circulation for martial arts that release great power in a single move. A pathway that added stability to explosive techniques. It perfectly matched the qi blade the Group Leader had shown.

*Divine skill.*

If he properly incorporated this method, it could greatly alleviate concerns about the rebound elasticity of Brilliant River -- the technique Azure Qilin Namgung Se-jin had named. An extremely valuable mental key.

"Look at this one."

A voice that could not be ignored. Strange amusement colouring every syllable.

"Now I see I've let quite the thief inside me."

The Divine Sword Group Leader's snicker came across as chilling despite its lightness. Of course he could feel Yeon-shin's qi sense scanning his interior. He was the Divine Sword Squad's greatest master. It would only be natural.

Yeon-shin did not apologize. There was no time.

*Since you were going to teach me one martial art anyway, if I ask forgiveness later...*

He removed his hand. The circulation had finished. Continuing was too dangerous with the Thirteen Evil Sects' masters closing in.

"Hey! Desolate Fortress' righteous fellows -- shall we have a brief word!"

A voice rang out from the third closest group, the qi behind it powerful enough to feel like someone shouting in their ears.

"Your contribution was great."

The Group Leader spoke softly. Then, with a smiling face, he ruffled Yeon-shin's hair.

And stepped forward.

His movements had become truly light. Some Divine Sword Squad masters stared at Yeon-shin in disbelief. Surprise equal to when he had returned after defeating Azure Qilin.

The Blood Flame Sect's blood demons and Deep Martial Alliance warriors faltered. They had no choice. Everyone had witnessed the terrifying might he had shown against the Namgung Family Head.

Energy rose. Divine qi. Fundamentally different -- not a matter of internal energy quantity but of *nature*. It rose thickly with each step, reminiscent of overwhelming disaster. He had brought the landslide with him.

*And I have to defeat that person...*

The Blood Flame Sect and Deep Martial Alliance changed course sharply. Their approach had been merely testing the waters.

But one group still came straight on. About thirty people. Yeon-shin's eyes narrowed.

*Martial Dragon Association. But their appearance and energy feel... strange. Masters from different sects gathered in one group. And no hostility.*

"We greet the Divine Swords of Desolate Fortress."

A delicate girl stepped forward. White cotton clothes over a light green silk palace dress. Ebony hair rippling to her waist. Jet-black against white skin, strikingly beautiful. Perhaps three years older than Yeon-shin.

Her energy swept over him. Upper dantian precognition -- uncomfortable, even surrounded by the Divine Sword Squad.

*Should I cut her down?*

---
